text: Could you get it to me by Thursday?

The migration and the training are in scope.
The custom reports are out of scope in this phase.
We will deliver the migration by June 20th. We will not deliver the mobile app.
Can we agree on two ground rules?
Before we start, let me be upfront about what is in scope and what is not.
We know the scope. Migration, training, reports. Let us move on.
Two standard reports are in. The custom reports are out of scope in this phase.
Wait. I assumed the custom reports were included. The client expects them.
That is exactly why I am saying it now, and not in September. They are not cancelled. We plan them for the autumn.
Fine. What else are you taking away from me?
Nothing. But I want two ground rules: no new requests after Friday, and one person who signs off on the design.
The client has a committee. They all want to decide.
Then the committee chooses one name. Ownership has to be with one person. Can we align on that today, in writing?
Let me be upfront about what is in and what is out.
They are not cancelled -- we plan them for the autumn.
Two ground rules: no new requests after Friday, and one decision-maker.
Can we agree on that today, in writing?

Diogo, the kickoff is at four, and I want one thing from you. Say the uncomfortable part out loud. Every project we lose, we lose because nobody said no at the beginning. The client will assume the custom reports are included. They are not. If you do not say it today, in front of everybody, then in September it becomes a conversation about money, and you will lose it. Say what is in, say what is out, and agree on who signs off. One person, not a committee. And put it on one page and send it the same day. If they say yes in writing, you have a project. If they do not, you have a problem -- and it is better to have it today.

Before we start the work, let me be upfront about the boundaries, because I would rather have this conversation today than in September. In scope for this phase: the migration, the training for your team, and two standard reports. Out of scope in this phase: the custom reports and the mobile app. They are not cancelled -- they are simply not in this phase, and we can plan them for the autumn. Two ground rules, if you agree. First: no new requests after Friday of each sprint, because a change on a Tuesday costs us a week. Second: one decision-maker on your side. Not a committee -- one person who signs off on the design by June 5th. Can we agree on that? I will send it in one page today, and I would like a yes in writing before we start.

Diogo opens the kickoff and says he wants to be upfront about the scope. He lists what is in: the migration, the training and two standard reports. Then he says what is out of scope in this phase: the custom reports. Sophia says she assumed they were included. Finally, Diogo proposes two ground rules and asks who signs off on the design.
